tasks.text: Scout Zone E — bush honeysuckle leafs out BEFORE natives; treat now while easy to spot
Cut and stump-treat honeysuckle (Zone E) and mulberry (Zone B) before full leaf-out
Mow/clear Zone G edges to reduce turf competition around seedlings
Scout Zone B for garlic mustard — treat before it goes to seed (May critical)
Treat blue spruces (Zone D) for Rhizosphaera when new growth is 1–2 inches (Daconil)
Prune Island lilacs (Zone F) after bloom — spring OK, avoid September
Flag and protect the young bur oak in Zone E NW corner
Order summer plants for Zone E riparian natives (silky dogwood, buttonbush, swamp milkweed)
Update Field Observation Log with any winter damage or new observations
Re-treat honeysuckle regrowth in Zone E — cut and paint stumps every 6 weeks all season
Re-treat mulberry sprouts in Zone B — cut at ground level and paint stumps
Mow Zone G to 4–6 inches — suppresses weeds without harming prairie seedlings
Plant riparian natives in Zone E along creek bank
Second Daconil spray on blue spruces if Rhizosphaera pressure remains high
Identify and photograph any unknown plants across the property
Document Zone G seedling progress — photograph and log observed species
Collect seed from desirable native wildflowers for fall broadcast seeding
Final honeysuckle treatment before hard freeze — glyphosate most effective in fall
Broadcast native seed on Zone G and disturbed areas in Zone A
Do NOT prune lilacs (Zone F) — fall pruning removes next year's flower buds
Leave seedheads standing on all native plants — winter bird food and natural reseeding
Stake and mark problem invasive areas for priority spring treatment
Update Zone Status Tracker Google Sheet with end-of-season progress
Plant balled-and-burlapped natives if available — fall is ideal for establishment
Order seeds and plants for next season from reputable Iowa native nurseries
Review Field Observation Log — assess what changed, update zone restoration goals
Plan spring invasive treatment schedule — prioritize Zone E and Zone B
Research keystone native species appropriate for each zone
Sharpen and maintain tools: loppers, pruners, brush saw, backpack sprayer
Stock up on Daconil and concentrated glyphosate before spring rush
